theorem_statement:
Gershgorin circle theorem.

Let \(A=(a_{ij})\) be a complex \(n\times n\) matrix. For each row index \(i\), define the Gershgorin disc
\[
D_i := \left\{ z \in \mathbb{C} : |z-a_{ii}| \le \sum_{j\ne i} |a_{ij}| \right\}.
\]
Then every eigenvalue \(\lambda\) of \(A\) lies in at least one of these discs. In other words, if \(Ax=\lambda x\) for some nonzero vector \(x\), then there exists an index \(r\) such that
\[
|\lambda-a_{rr}| \le \sum_{j\ne r} |a_{rj}|.
\]
Hence
\[
\operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq \bigcup_{i=1}^n D_i.
\]
raw_proof_text:
We prove that every eigenvalue of a complex matrix lies in at least one Gershgorin disc.

Let \(A=(a_{ij})\) be a complex \(n\times n\) matrix, and let \(\lambda\in\mathbb{C}\) be an eigenvalue of \(A\). By definition, there exists a nonzero vector
\[
x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)^T \in \mathbb{C}^n
\]
such that
\[
Ax=\lambda x.
\]
We must show that there is some index \(r\) for which
\[
|\lambda-a_{rr}| \le \sum_{j\ne r} |a_{rj}|.
\]

Because \(x\neq 0\), at least one component of \(x\) is nonzero. Choose an index \(r\) such that
\[
|x_r| = \max_{1\le j\le n} |x_j|.
\]
Then \(|x_r|\ge |x_j|\) for every \(j\), and in particular \(x_r\neq 0\), since otherwise all \(|x_j|\) would be zero and hence \(x=0\), contrary to assumption.

Now look at the \(r\)-th coordinate of the eigenvalue equation \(Ax=\lambda x\). The \(r\)-th row gives
\[
\sum_{j=1}^n a_{rj}x_j = \lambda x_r.
\]
Separate the diagonal term from the rest:
\[
a_{rr}x_r + \sum_{j\ne r} a_{rj}x_j = \lambda x_r.
\]
Rearranging, we get
\[
(\lambda-a_{rr})x_r = \sum_{j\ne r} a_{rj}x_j.
\]
Since \(x_r\neq 0\), we may divide by \(x_r\):
\[
\lambda-a_{rr} = \sum_{j\ne r} a_{rj}\frac{x_j}{x_r}.
\]
Take absolute values and apply the triangle inequality:
\[
|\lambda-a_{rr}|
\le
\sum_{j\ne r} \left|a_{rj}\frac{x_j}{x_r}\right|
=
\sum_{j\ne r} |a_{rj}|\frac{|x_j|}{|x_r|}.
\]
But by the choice of \(r\), we have \(|x_j|\le |x_r|\) for every \(j\). Therefore
\[
\frac{|x_j|}{|x_r|} \le 1
\qquad\text{for all } j.
\]
Substituting this into the previous inequality yields
\[
|\lambda-a_{rr}|
\le
\sum_{j\ne r} |a_{rj}|.
\]
This is exactly the statement that
\[
\lambda \in D_r,
\]
where
\[
D_r = \left\{ z\in\mathbb{C} : |z-a_{rr}| \le \sum_{j\ne r}|a_{rj}| \right\}.
\]
Thus every eigenvalue \(\lambda\) belongs to at least one Gershgorin disc.

Since \(\lambda\) was an arbitrary eigenvalue of \(A\), we conclude that the spectrum of \(A\) is contained in the union of the Gershgorin discs:
\[
\operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq \bigcup_{i=1}^n D_i.
\]
This proves the theorem.
